data.body:
PASS 1 review (A1 anti-slop / over-engineering / NIH): CLEAN — no findings.

Affirmations:
- No new abstraction/dependency/config introduced. The plan is documentation-only ("This is documentation only — no behavior changes") and explicitly REUSES three existing subsystems rather than rebuilding them: `rebar.signing` (sign_manifest@src/rebar/signing.py:319, resolve_prompt@src/rebar/llm/prompts.py:648, get_prompt@src/rebar/llm/prompts.py:173, run_workflow@src/rebar/llm/workflow/executor.py:584, validate_structured@src/rebar/llm/findings.py:247 all verified present). This is the opposite of NIH — it documents the reuse seam.
- YAGNI: serves a current done-definition (reuse reference for the plan-review gate epic), not a hypothetical.
- No config-surface proliferation: no new config keys proposed.
- No golden-hammer / cargo-cult / resume-driven / premature-optimization signals — it documents existing machinery against the live source with a CI anti-drift gate (test_reuse_surface_doc.py), which avoids duplicating doc-vs-code rather than adding redundant tooling.
